title: Which baseline file layout best preserves git-diff readability when adding a bro
question: Which baseline file layout best preserves git-diff readability when adding a browser axis: flat {slug}-{browser}-{viewport}.png, per-browser subdirectories (.cloverleaf/baselines/{browser}/{slug}-{viewport}.png), or per-slug bundles?
findings: Current baseline convention (ui-reviewer.md line 78, confirmed by actual files in .cloverleaf/baselines/) is a flat layout: {slug}-{viewport}.png, e.g. faq-desktop.png, faq-mobile.png, guide-tablet.png. Git history (commits 6120e72, 365739f, d09d5ce) shows these are committed as a cohesive group when all viewports for a route change together — the flat list reads cleanly today because there are only 3 viewports and 1 browser. Adding a browser axis multiplies the flat file count by the browser count: 3 browsers × 3 viewports × N routes = 9N PNGs in a single directory. In git diff --stat or a GitHub PR file tree this becomes unwieldy — entries like faq-chromium-desktop.png, faq-chromium-mobile.png, faq-chromium-tablet.png, faq-firefox-desktop.png, faq-firefox-mobile.png, faq-firefox-tablet.png, faq-webkit-desktop.png, faq-webkit-mobile.png, faq-webkit-tablet.png interleave alphabetically by slug, not by browser, making it hard to scan browser-specific regressions. SlugifyRoute (route-slug.ts) already uses hyphens as its separator, so flat names collide with slug segments: a route /guide-chapter/ at viewport desktop in webkit would produce guide-chapter-webkit-desktop.png — ambiguous whether webkit is part of the slug or the browser field. Per-browser subdirectories (.cloverleaf/baselines/{browser}/{slug}-{viewport}.png) solve all three problems: (1) git diff groups additions/modifications under a single browser prefix, so adding webkit shows as a clean A baselines/webkit/ subtree; (2) removing or renaming a browser is a single directory removal with no risk of partial deletions; (3) the {slug}-{viewport}.png name within each subdir is identical to the current convention, preserving backward compatibility for chromium baselines by symlinking or migrating in one step. compareVisual in lib/visual-diff.ts takes an arbitrary baselinePath string and calls mkdirSync with recursive:true, so it supports any depth without code changes — only the path-construction call-site in the UI Reviewer prompt needs updating. Per-slug bundles (a subdir per route: baselines/{slug}/{browser}-{viewport}.png or baselines/{slug}/{browser}/{viewport}.png) add an extra nesting level that offers no advantage over per-browser subdirs for the browser-axis use case and would require changing both the path construction and the attachment labels in the reviewer output schema. The existing .cloverleaf/baselines/ flat files for chromium would need to be migrated to baselines/chromium/ at the point the RFC is implemented; this is a one-time rename and is best done as an explicit migration step in the implementation task rather than a compatibility shim, since the files are tracked in git and the rename is a clean R (rename) operation.
recommendation: Adopt per-browser subdirectories: .cloverleaf/baselines/{browser}/{slug}-{viewport}.png. This layout preserves the existing {slug}-{viewport}.png filename convention within each browser directory, keeps git diffs scannable by browser (additions and regressions are grouped under baselines/chromium/, baselines/webkit/, baselines/firefox/ respectively), eliminates slug/browser token ambiguity that would arise with flat {slug}-{browser}-{viewport}.png names when slugifyRoute produces hyphenated slugs, and requires no changes to compareVisual — only the baselinePath construction in the UI Reviewer prompt and the attachment label paths in the output schema need updating. For the RFC/Plan: (1) update the UI Reviewer prompt's step 6 to construct baselinePath as {{repo_root}}/.cloverleaf/baselines/{browser}/{slug}-{viewport}.png and iterate browser as the outermost loop; (2) include a one-time migration task that git mv's existing flat baselines into baselines/chromium/; (3) update the attachment path template in the output schema to use the new subdir form. The per-slug bundle option is not recommended — it adds nesting depth without improving browser-axis readability and departs further from the current convention.
